attributes.description: Gateway connect/disconnect events. The simplest signal for link health on the LoRaWAN edge — sustained noise here usually points to flaky backhaul or power on the gateway.
attributes.title: TTS — Gateway link health

attributes.description: Join activity across all applications. event_action distinguishes accept vs reject; pair with event_component:js for join-server-side detail.
attributes.title: TTS — Joins

attributes.description: Anything the stack dropped — uplinks, downlinks, application messages. A spike here is almost always actionable: bad payload formatter, queue overflow, or a misconfigured integration.
attributes.title: TTS — Drops

attributes.description: Identity Server OAuth and user events — login, token issue, key creation. Use this as your audit trail for who-did-what on the console and API.

kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON: {"query":{"query":"name : (oauth.* or account.* or user.* or invitation.* or *.api-key.* or *.collaborator.*)","language":"kuery"},"filter":[],"indexRefName":"kibanaSavedObjectMeta.searchSourceJSON.index"}

attributes.title: TTS — Auth and audit

attributes.description: Uplinks the application server actually forwarded — what your application code saw. Compare against ns.up.receive to diagnose where uplinks get lost between the gateway and your integration.
attributes.title: TTS — Application uplinks
